scanner_blind_spots.reason: Scanner screened out on 2% revenue growth (DOJ overhang distortion). THREE independent experts (homer, antonis, insider_flow) flagged independently. CEO $25M + CFO $5M + 10 directors = largest cluster buy in mega-cap insurance. Q1 beat+raise proves business intact. Scanner's quantitative filter missed qualitative insider conviction signal.

Scanner screened out on 220x P/E. TWO experts (geopolitics, ai_technology) independently flagged Army $10B + commercial +137% YoY. May 4 earnings are a binary catalyst. Scanner valuation screens reject high-multiple names regardless of growth quality — missing the AI/defense supercycle thesis.

Scanner screened out on 44.2x fwd P/E. Q1 2026 was the best industrial quarter of 2026: +71% orders, $163B backlog, data center equipment orders exceeding all of 2025. Scanner PE screens can't detect when earnings are about to re-rate dramatically upward.

Scanner missed a binary FDA PDUFA event (May 10, 9 days out) with clean Phase 3 data (p=0.0068) and Priority Review. The scanner can't read the FDA calendar, match it to clinical trial endpoints, and assess scientific significance. $1,029 analyst target vs ~$780 stock = 32% upside with known catalyst date.

New CFO bought 22,453 shares ($1.6M) 9 days into the role — his first-ever open-market purchase. This insider conviction signal (new executive betting immediately on entry price) isn't quantifiable by a scanner. 52 analysts covering with $105 avg target (+41% upside) adds validation.

Scanner likely screened out due to low beta (0.29) and modest FCF yield flag. But XOM is the most direct beneficiary of the Hormuz blockade narrative — Brent $108, Q1 earnings reporting today. The scanner's quantitative bias against low-beta names missed the highest-visibility macro trade of the week.
